data.body: Security hardening in the adopted code: LLM-Review flagged a token-purpose-confusion auth-bypass in the inherited auth lib (auth/lib/cookie.js). signState() and cookie.sign() both HMAC with the SAME cookie secret; verifyState() requires payload.p=='state' but cookie.verify() checked only HMAC+exp, and the edge gate grants access on res.valid alone. A 'state' token (exposed in the /authorize redirect URL) could thus replay as a session cookie (identity-less bypass). Fixed cookie.verify() to reject any purpose-labeled token (session cookies carry no 'p'); backward-compatible (live sessions unaffected). Re-applied (auth-host Lambda bundle redeployed; auth host does not call verify() so its behavior is unchanged) — plan clean again; SSO still 302s to Google. Not currently exploitable in rebar (no edge-gate instance deployed), but hardened before the module's first use.
